overview: Boros Bully is a competitive Pauper Aggro deck that leverages low-cost creatures, protection spells, and graveyard recursion to secure quick victories. It adapts to the metagame by including specific maindeck answers for popular strategies like Affinity, Faeries, and Elves. Success relies on careful resource management and precise timing rather than simply flooding the board.

keep_criteria: Hands with early creatures and lands
Keep any hand with 2-3 lands (mix of Plains/Mountains) and at least one of Faithless Looting, Kor Skyfisher, or Thraben Charm.
Prioritize hands that can play a turn-1 land and a turn-2 threat or discard spell.
mulligan_criteria: Hands full of lands paired with Faithless Looting
Hands lacking early threats or card advantage
Mulligan hands with 0-1 lands, three+ non-basic lands, or hands lacking early threats/interaction.

examples.reason: Squadron Hawk is one of the best cards to have in your starting hand, especially if you're on a Mulligan.

early_game: Play low-cost creatures quickly to establish board presence
Avoid opening with Faithless Looting on turn 1 to preserve information and graveyard options
Turn 2: Play a second land, then cast Faithless Looting to dig for a Kor Skyfisher or Thraben Charm, or play a cheap two-drop if available.
If you draw Skyfisher on turn 2, cast it immediately for the flying attacker and 2 damage to stabilize or finish.
Sequence lands to ensure double-colored mana is available for Skyfisher and Charm by turn 3.
mid_game: Hold Prismatic Strands to protect against sweepers and red removal
Cast Battle Screech to generate value and board presence
Discard Dust to Dust if not facing Affinity to find better answers
Faithless Looting is your primary card advantage engine
use it when your hand is empty or you need a specific answer/threat.
Don't overcast it if you're at 3+ cards and have a clear path to win.
Manage mana to keep Thraben Charm available for key turns.
The graveyard is a resource for discard effects
late_game: Recast Rally the Peasants to finish the game quickly
Maintain land count and board control to outvalue opponents
Kor Skyfisher with flying and haste is your primary win condition, supported by direct damage and discard to clear the path.
Thraben Charm can exile key blockers or sweep minor threats to enable a lethal attack.
Go all-in when you have a clear path to lethal (3+ damage away), opponent has no sweepers or hard removal in hand, or you've discarded their key answers.
Also commit when you can trade Skyfisher into a blocker and still have lethal next turn.

key_cards.name: Squadron Hawk
key_cards.role: Early game threat and card advantage
key_cards.notes: One of the best cards to have in your starting hand; resolves to put a flying creature on the board and 'draw' three other creatures.

key_cards.name: Rally the Peasants
key_cards.notes: Used to win many times in just one attack; can be recast from the graveyard to finish games.

key_cards.name: Prismatic Strands
key_cards.notes: Protects creatures from sweepers and red removal; essential against Goblins Combo and Faeries.

key_cards.name: Dust to Dust
key_cards.notes: Crucial against Affinity; can be discarded early in other matchups to find answers or reused from the graveyard.

key_cards.name: Electrickery
key_cards.notes: Excellent against Faeries, Elves, and the mirror match; can end games when boards are locked.

key_cards.name: Thrilling Discovery
key_cards.notes: Draws two cards and can discard them, improving odds of finding answers and fueling the graveyard recursion mechanic.

key_cards.name: Lunarch Veteran // Luminous Phantom
key_cards.notes: Helps against Burn and aggro decks by providing early board presence and flying.

key_cards.name: Battle Screech
key_cards.notes: Creates 4 creatures with just one card, providing massive value and attacking pressure.

sequencing_tips: Avoid playing Faithless Looting on turn 1 to keep information and potential graveyard reuse
Prioritize resolving Squadron Hawk early to flood the board
Hold Prismatic Strands to protect against sweepers and red removal
Discard Dust to Dust early if not facing Affinity to find answers
Use Electrickery carefully in the mirror to break locked boards
Common mistake: overusing Faithless Looting when you already have a full hand, leaving you vulnerable to discard or sweepers.
Always sequence plays to ensure you can cast Skyfisher and Charm on the same turn if needed.
Thraben Charm's exile mode is strictly better than destruction against artifact/enchantment decks.
card advantage and efficient trades win these.

matchups.advice: Use Dust to Dust right away, preferably at the start of the game. Make as many creatures as possible to force land spending and stay ahead.
Use flying creatures to avoid Ninjutsu. Protect Squadron Hawk and Battle Screech with counters or sweepers. Side in Parapet against Dimir and Prismatic Strands against Izzet.
Go by quantity and try to win in one swing with Rally the Peasants. Use Electrickery to clear the board and Prismatic Strands for protection.
Play creatures as quickly as possible to prevent board establishment, but keep lands in play. Resolve high-cost cards like Battle Screech and Rally the Peasants to finish.
Start with Squadron Hawk and play Battle Screech on turn 4. Expect a locked board with Prismatic Strands and lootings; use Electrickery to break the stalemate.

Use discard to remove counterspells from hand. Cast threats in response to non-removal spells or when opponent is tapped out. Use Thraben Charm's exile mode on their counterspell if it's a creature (unlikely) or focus on applying pressure they can't interact with.

Apply maximum early pressure to race them. Use discard to hit their ramp spells or key threats. Thraben Charm can exile their early ramp sources if they're creatures. Don't trade up unnecessarily; win before they stabilize.

Stabilize with Thraben Charm's exile mode on key early threats. Use discard to disrupt their hand. Play Skyfisher to block flying threats or trade into bodies. Prioritize survival over aggression until you stabilize.

Pressure relentlessly with early threats. Use discard to hit their answers or combo pieces. Thraben Charm can remove their key blockers or sweepers. Don't let them stabilize; keep applying damage and disruption.

Win the discard war. Prioritize keeping hands with multiple threats and interaction. Use Thraben Charm to exile their Skyfisher or key blockers. Trade efficiently and recognize when to concede a trade to gain card advantage.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells (hold up interaction or cast threats in response to non-removal spells), sweepers (hold up Charm or discard to disrupt setup), and graveyard strategies (discard to empty their library/graveyard).
Note when opponents tap out for big spells

overview_evidence.evidence_span: It is an Aggro with good answers and protection for its creatures.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: winning many times in just one attack [with Rally the Peasants]
Squadron Hawk... we will 'draw' three other creatures.
Battle Screech to create 4 creatures with just one card

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: I don't particularly like opening a handful of lands with Faithless Looting and discarding and drawing other cards right on turn 1.
we have to know how to spend our resources at the right time.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Affinity has a card that is incredibly troublesome for us, Makeshift Munitions
Dimir has Echoing Decay and/or Suffocating Fumes , and from Izzet has Electrickery and/or Fiery Cannonade
with Jaspera Sentinel , things got complicated, as this creature blocks ours incredibly well.
